title: Melanie Klein Among the U-Boats
description:
‘Whenever Richard mentioned Hitler, Klein unwaveringly substituted “Hitler” with “penis”.’

Ben Parker on Melanie Klein and child psychology.

The post Melanie Klein Among the U-Boats appeared first on Granta.
hook: Melanie Klein replaced Hitler with penis in every sentence. A biography of how trauma rewrites the mind's vocabulary.

title: Love in a Fallen City: Shanghai’s Marriage Market
description: “‘Oi,’ he whispered. I caught the whites of his eyes above his frames. ‘I’ve got a ’95 man. Want to see? Most of my clients are at least 1.85 meters.’”
hook: Shanghai's marriage market where men are ranked by height and birth year like livestock. A brutal glimpse at love as transaction.

title: Are There More Bunnies in Toronto?
description: "I never used to see rabbits in Toronto. Then they were everywhere. Then I lost my mind."
hook: Toronto's rabbit explosion drove one person to obsession—a love letter to how animals infiltrate our cities and our sanity.

title: Artist of sympathy and cruelty
description:
Mozart’s genius lay in writing music of such power that he could draw his audience into morally wrenching predicaments

- by Dorian Bandy
hook: Mozart's gift wasn't beauty—it was cruelty. On genius as the ability to wound and seduce simultaneously.

title: A Debut Novel That Exposes the Ugliness of American Subjectivity
description:
Bobuq Sayed’s début, No God but Us, reinvents the modern American Abroad novel––the story, now over a century old, of Americans departing the US and crossing an ocean to find freedom and growth that they could not access at home. From Langston Hughes to Ernest Hemingway, James Baldwin to Garth Greenwell, the American Abroad novel […]

The post A Debut Novel That Exposes the U
hook: A debut that explodes the American Abroad myth—no liberation found, only the ugliness you bring with you.

title: For a Minute there… Radiohead’s Amnesiac a Quarter of a Century on
description:
Toby Manning examines the cultural amnesia surrounding one of Radiohead's best (if sometimes forgotten about) albums

The fact that Amnesiac has been subject to a cultural amnesia is less ironic than apposite. Like the fabled millennium bug, Radiohead's 2001 hit album was an expression of doubt and trepidation at the dawning of a new, digital century
hook: Radiohead's Amnesiac aged better than Kid A, but we've all forgotten to listen. On cultural amnesia and underrated masterpieces.

title: Nicola Florimbi’s Paintings Are Unsettling and Necessary
description: Her depictions of individuals in settings that seem both out of time and of this moment represent one of many engaging paradoxes.
hook: Nicola Florimbi paints people suspended between eras. They haunt you because you can't locate them in time.
